idea: App-mediated delegated wallet access for x402 and MPP so users can fund a wallet once, grant tools permission via sign-in links, and enforce spend limits without sharing private keys in sandboxes.

demand_signals: Coinbase Payments MCP already targets the same pain point: payments without API keys, seed phrases, or manual intervention.
x402 V2 added wallet-controlled sessions and reusable access on December 11, 2025, validating the need for delegated repeat-use access.
Stripe machine payments documentation now supports x402 on Solana and MPP on Tempo and card rails, confirming the category is becoming real infrastructure rather than a one-off experiment.

risks.description: Coinbase Payments MCP already offers an embedded wallet, funding flow, and spending limits, so a generic wallet app is not a strong wedge.
Turnkey, Privy, and similar providers already expose wallet policy engines and authorization controls that cover much of the technical moat.
Users must believe the app cannot overreach permissions or leak signing authority across tools.
MPP is still early and surrounding tool patterns are changing quickly.

next_steps: Position the product as a cross-client agent payment permission broker, not a standalone wallet.
Build integration-first using existing wallet infrastructure such as Turnkey, Privy, Crossmint, or Coinbase embedded wallets.
Test a fake-door flow for grant links, spend caps, revocation, and per-tool authorization before building a full wallet product.
Interview at least 8 users who currently run agent tools with local keys or wallet files and document exact failure modes and trust objections.
Only proceed if at least 3 users prefer one permissioned wallet identity across multiple tools over existing single-product embedded wallets.
